Speaker 1: Hi, guests. Welcome back. Here we are going to explore Themeplate 6, which is
Slaughterhouse V. We're going to be using this themeplate when you're using an email
only minimum viable funnel to really drive home those ahas and beliefships.
This one will mirror that classic PASOP, P-A-S-O-P, structure. So if you want to see more
of that one, in [inaudible 00:00:27] emails, there is a lesson on that. Otherwise, Joanna
has this epic post that also talks a little more about it. But essentially what we're going
to be doing is we're going to use that structure and integrate the principles of those
sacred cows and ahas that are so vital and essential to the pre-launch phase.
This is how the container is going to go for it. We're going to call out one sacred cow.
We're going to agitate by describing the sour milk it produces. This is like a more specific
moment of highest tension. Think of it if you just drank some sour milk. That will be a
moment of highest tension that is very specific to drinking that particular sour milk.
Then we're going to propose a new more empowering and believable aha that counters
it and describe the benefits and opportunity in adopting this belief. You do want to sell
that new belief, so it's important to actually show the benefit around it.
Then we can open up a new problem that relates to their ability to adopt and integrate
that piece. That will set up that open movement continuation to the next email. So the
PASOP structure meets the sacred cows. PASOP, if you just want a brief interview ...
Brief interview ... brief overview is problem agitation, that's the sour milk, solution,
which is the new belief, opportunity, which stems from the new belief, and then you set
up the new problem, the new sacred cow that you're going to talk about in the next
email.
An example of this is can yoga teachers really make a living? It's question based, and
one that is really present and urgent for this market. Then you just cut straight to it. All
right. This might not win me friends, but it has to be said. Right off the bat, you're giving
that disclaimer that you're going to be challenging. That gets attention. The reason
you're still not making it as a yoga teacher is because, well, you're still trying to make it
as a yoga teacher. That's that big macro sacred cow.
Think about it for a sec. If you're still scrambling around running from studio to studio,
desperately pleading with every stingy studio to pay you a livable wage that wouldn't
force you to moonlighting as a Starbucks barista, then maybe it's time for a new
definition. This is that sour milk, which is our way of agitating the pain that the flawed
belief is causing them. We want to create that causality and linkage between the flawed
beliefs and the moment of highest tension, as this creates that diagnosis and the feeling
that the situation can be shifted.
When that doesn't induce those unyogic reactions every time a well-meaning BFF or
parent has the audacity to ask you how the business is going. This is really grounding it
in their reality. "I've worn the same Lulus 37 days in a row. How the hell do you think it's
going?" Specificity. So if this whole yoga teacher thing is just an endless practice of
stress, self-doubt, and stinky yoga pants, why wouldn't you be open to changing the job
description?

Now we're going to get into selling that new belief, one that empowers you to serve on
a deeper more sustainable level, one that doesn't force you into a veiled smile every
time someone questions your career choices, one that actually gives you a sip of this
whole freedom thing that every yogi with an Instagram account has been going apeshit
over for the last half decade.
The truth is there are thousands of yoga professionals who live this reality each and
every day, so that's really that opportunity. But they didn't get there by submitting to
penny pinching studio owners or trying to see if the green Starbucks apron matches
their yoga pants. Specificity. They got there by first declaring themselves as a yoga
entrepreneur. So now we're just reinforcing that big shift, that big paradigm shift we
want them to make and the benefits of doing it, and then learning the practical business
skills and mindset shifts that open the floodgates to a deeper well of abundance and
service. They didn't rely on mantras, but the powerful marketing strategies that quickly
positioned them as an in-demand yoga professional who gets to put hustle into
permanent savasana.
Here we just recap. Yoga teacher to yoga entrepreneur. Now we're actually countering
the objection for the next sacred cow. "So great, I get it, but I suck at all that marketing
stuff. I once tried to set up a Squarespace account, and I swear I nearly broke the
internet." Once again, this is pulled directly from what that audience is doing. A lot of
them have known about marketing. It's not like they're totally blind to it, and they don't
have WordPress skills, so they'll go on one off those site builders, which seems really,
really easy to do when you read the home page for one of those site builders, and then
you try to do it, and it's just pixelized hell. That's very familiar and grounded in their
reality, and you get this through your data mining, through your conversations, through
your interviews with your avatar and your research and your intuition.
Don't underestimate your own intuition. Don't overestimate it all the time, but
definitely don't underestimate it. That's a topic for our next email. This crazy
misperception that you need to be everywhere to have a thriving business. Stay tuned
for another email coming later this week that will show you a much simpler framework
for marketing your business. So we're opening that loop that's going to counter that
next sacred cow. Till then, sign off.
The other thing I really want to know. This is a belief they're so willing to let go of,
because it's not serving them. They're not great at that tech stuff, and they really don't
want to be everywhere. They just don't want to hold onto that belief, so they're just so
much more willing to let it go.
Till then, sign off, and then you could kind of add that PS that reflects a little bit of your
story and your experience with that sacred cow. I actually didn't even build my website
until after I made it as a yoga entrepreneur. Instead, I armed myself with three core
strategies that immediately allowed me to work less, earn more, and gain leverage to
